title: Verify and test: task tier classification + session cleanup
context: All implementation is complete (see docs/task-tier-session-cleanup-design.md). Previous architect runs confirmed: (1) app/services/task_tier.py classifies at task creation, (2) model_chooser.py uses explicit tier from DB settings, (3) worker_gateway.py uses WebSocket sessions.delete, (4) workflow_seeds.py has session_refs on all spawn nodes. This is a verification and bugfix task — run the tests, confirm it works, fix any issues found.
acceptance: 1. POST /api/tasks returns task with model_tier populated (never null). 2. lobs-server tasks always get minimum 'standard' tier. 3. After a task completes, its worker session is deleted from Gateway (verify with openclaw sessions list or gateway logs). 4. Session count stays low over multiple task cycles (no leak). 5. Logs show '[GATEWAY] Deleted session' after each workflow completion.
notes: If WebSocket sessions.delete is rejected by Gateway (check logs for auth errors), investigate REST alternative: DELETE http://localhost:18789/api/sessions/{key} with Bearer token. The WebSocket handshake uses challenge-response auth — if the 'role': 'operator' scope is wrong, that would cause silent failures. Fix the scope or switch to REST if needed.
